title: Essential Coffee in Lower Manhattan
blurb: The roasters, the third-wave standbys, and the places that have earned their regulars.

places.name: Cafe Integral
places.desc: Family-owned roaster. The cortado is their calling card — they rotate seasonally. Small space with focused energy. Cash appreciated.

places.name: Abraço
places.desc: A five-stool espresso bar in the East Village that's been doing things right since 2007. The olive oil cake is the move. Arrive early — they run out.

places.name: Russ & Daughters Cafe
places.desc: The cafe offshoot of the legendary appetizing shop. Smoked fish, bagels, and coffee in a beautiful room. More seats than the original. Weekend waits are real.

places.name: Dimes Market
places.desc: Grain bowls, great coffee, natural wines, and a market with things you actually want to cook. Sunday brunch line moves fast. Unhurried vibe.

places.name: Kopitiam
places.desc: Malaysian coffee shop doing kopi the traditional way — dark roast with condensed milk, kaya toast on the side. A completely different register than third-wave espresso. Worth the detour.
